title: Initialize Payment
description: Creates a new mobile money checkout transaction. Vesicash returns a hosted payment_url to which you redirect the customer — it handles the mobile money prompt and 3DS entirely.
auth: Requires a live x-api-key (secret key) in the request header. See the Authentication guide for key management.

params.name: amount
params.description: The transaction amount in the **smallest currency unit**. For Ghana, pass pesewas (1 GHS = 100 pesewas); for Nigeria, pass kobo (1 NGN = 100 kobo); for Zambia, pass ngwe (1 ZMW = 100 ngwe). Must be a positive integer.

params.name: country
params.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for the transaction. Determines which banking network Vesicash routes through. Example: `GH` for Ghana, `NG` for Nigeria, `ZM` for Zambia, `KE` for Kenya.

params.name: currency
params.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Must match the country you specified. Example: `GHS` for Ghana Cedis, `NGN` for Nigerian Naira, `ZMW` for Zambian Kwacha.

params.name: method
params.description: The payment channel to use. Set to `"mobilemoney"` for mobile money checkout.

params.name: narration
params.description: A short description of what the customer is paying for. Displayed on the Vesicash checkout page and included on the customer's bank statement. Maximum 100 characters.

params.name: redirect_url
params.description: A publicly accessible URL on your server. Vesicash redirects the customer here after the payment completes (success or failure). Must be HTTPS in production.

params.name: webhook_url
params.description: A publicly accessible HTTPS endpoint on your server. Vesicash POSTs a signed JSON payload to this URL whenever the transaction status changes. Always verify the signature using your webhook secret.

params.name: customer_email
params.description: Customer's email address. Used for receipt delivery and fraud screening.

params.name: customer_phone_number
params.description: Customer's phone number in international format (no `+`). Example: `260968832801` for a Zambian number.

responseSchema.name: success
responseSchema.description: `true` when the transaction was initialized successfully.

responseSchema.name: code
responseSchema.description: HTTP status code echoed in the response body. `201` on success.

responseSchema.name: data.uuid
responseSchema.description: Vesicash's internal unique identifier for this transaction.

responseSchema.name: data.reference
responseSchema.description: Vesicash-generated payment reference. Use this to query transaction status via `GET /api/v1/transactions/:reference`.

responseSchema.name: data.merchant_reference
responseSchema.description: Your own reference passed in the request, echoed back for reconciliation.

responseSchema.name: data.status
responseSchema.description: Initial transaction status. Typically `"pending"` immediately after initialization.

responseSchema.name: data.payment_url
responseSchema.description: The hosted Vesicash checkout URL. Redirect your customer here to complete the mobile money payment.

responseSchema.name: data.environment
responseSchema.description: `"live"` or `"test"` depending on the API key used.

errors.description: The `amount` field is missing, zero, or not a positive integer.
The `currency` does not match the supported currencies for the specified `country`.
The `customer_phone_number` is not a valid international-format number for the specified country.
The `x-api-key` header is missing, expired, or not a valid live key.
A transaction with this `Idempotency-Key` was already created. Return the original response instead of retrying.
An unexpected server-side error occurred. Retry with exponential backoff or contact support.

blocks.content: After the customer completes payment, Vesicash redirects to your `redirect_url` and sends a signed webhook to your `webhook_url`. **Always verify the outcome server-side** using `GET /api/v1/transactions/:reference` — never rely solely on the redirect.

description.content: Creates a new mobile money checkout transaction. Vesicash returns a hosted payment_url to which you redirect the customer — it handles the mobile money prompt and 3DS entirely.
